article_title: Nine Weeks of Immunotherapy Before Surgery Leaves Every Bowel Cancer Patient Relapse-Free After 33 Months in NEOPRISM-CRC Trial
summary: Submission approved: all substantive claims verified. Word count marginally over Briefing limit (1024/1000) but content quality justifies approval. Allowlist warnings are false positives. medicalxpress.com 403 is not a problem — claim confirmed in UCL News.

findings.message: Body too long for Briefing: 1024 words (maximum 1000)

content_preview.title: Nine Weeks of Immunotherapy Before Surgery Leaves Every Bowel Cancer Patient Relapse-Free After 33 Months in NEOPRISM-CRC Trial
content_preview.summary: A UK trial of preoperative pembrolizumab for MMR-deficient colorectal cancer shows zero relapses across all 32 patients after nearly three years of follow-up.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A small but striking clinical trial has kept every one of its participants free of cancer recurrence for nearly three years — an outcome that researchers say could reshape how a specific subset of bowel cancer is treated. The NEOPRISM-CRC trial, led by investigators at University College London and University College London Hospitals, replaced standard post-operative chemotherapy with nine weeks of the immunotherapy drug pembrolizumab administered before surgery. At a mean follow-up...

recommendations: Consider adding ecancer.org and insideprecisionmedicine.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — both are legitimate oncology publications.
Future submissions for detailed clinical trials may warrant the News category to avoid word count warnings.

editor_notes.content_quality: High-quality Briefing covering a significant clinical trial result. 1,024 words is 24 over the 1,000-word Briefing cap — a borderline case that does not warrant rejection given the substantive quality. The article appropriately caveats the small sample size and conference-abstract-only status.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read 5 of 6 source snapshots (medicalxpress.com returned HTTP 403 — no snapshot available). source-0.html.gz (sciencedaily.com): Confirmed NEOPRISM-CRC, 32 patients, 33 months, 0 relapses, 59% pCR, Kai-Keen Shiu title/quote, Yanrong Jiang title, Marnix Jansen title/quote, Christopher Burston patient story/quote verbatim. source-1.html.gz (ucl.ac.uk): Confirmed all same facts independently, plus 5 hospital names including Christie NHS Foundation Trust Manchester. source-2.html.gz (ecancer.org): Confirmed all trial facts independently. source-3.html.gz (insideprecisionmedicine.com): Confirmed ctDNA progression (24%→43%→58% after cycles 1-3→100% post-surgery), super/dynamic/poor molecular responder groups (6/11/8 patients), 100%/82%/0% pCR by group. source-5.html.gz (scitechdaily.com): Confirmed patient story, 5-year survival rates (90%/65%/10% by stage), all trial facts. medicalxpress.com (source-4): 403 blocked. Claim attributed to it ('25% relapse within 3 years under standard care') is confirmed in UCL News (source-1).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quantitative claims verified: 32 patients, 33 months mean follow-up, 0 relapses, 59% pCR, 10-15% MMR subtype, 44,000 UK cases/year, ctDNA progression percentages 24/43/58/100%, molecular responder groups confirmed. All researcher names, titles, and quotes verified verbatim.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and scientifically accurate. Appropriately cautious about the small sample size and conference-abstract status. No sensationalism despite striking results.
editor_notes.originality: No prior NEOPRISM-CRC coverage found in archive.
recommendations: Consider adding ecancer.org and insideprecisionmedicine.com to source allowlist.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent clinical trial Briefing. All claims verified against 5 of 6 sources; the one blocked source (medicalxpress.com) has its attributed claim confirmed in UCL News. APPROVE.
